question_title: Will Sony officially announce the start date of PlayStation Days of Play 2026 on the PlayStation Blog by May 27, 2026?
summary: Days of Play is a firmly established annual PlayStation event (~9 years running) that has always been announced on the PlayStation Blog. The key question is timing: will the 2026 announcement fall by May 27? Historical announcements track the 4th Tuesday of May, which in 2026 is May 26 — comfortably within the deadline. In 3 of 4 recent years, announcements landed on this date. The main NO scenario is a 2023-like delay (announcement May 28+), which happened once in four years. The Days of Play landing page already showing a 2026 title and unanimous industry speculation pointing to the week of May 26 further support a timely announcement. Even a 1-day slip to May 27 still resolves YES. I estimate 82% probability of YES.

factors.description: Days of Play is a deeply established annual tradition (~9 years), always announced on PlayStation Blog. Landing page already updated to '2026'. Near-certain the event will happen and be announced on blog.

Calendar alignment: The 4th Tuesday of May 2026 is May 26 — within the May 27 deadline. In 3/4 recent years (2022, 2024, 2025), announcements fell on the 4th Tuesday. The dominant pattern predicts a May 26 announcement.
Industry outlets (PlayStation LifeStyle, Startselect, G3Geek) unanimously expect event starting week of May 26, consistent with 'last Wednesday of May' pattern.

2023 outlier risk: In 2023, announcement was May 29 with sale starting June 2. A similar ~6-day delay from the 4th Tuesday (May 26) would put announcement on June 1 — well past deadline. This is the primary NO scenario.
Buffer: Even a 1-day slip from May 26 to May 27 still resolves YES. Blog posts publish at ~5 AM PDT (12 PM UTC), well before the 23:59 UTC deadline on May 27.

reasoning:
## Forecast: **82% probability YES**

### Key Reasoning

**Days of Play** is Sony's annual sales event, running consistently since ~2017, always announced on the PlayStation Blog. The central question is whether the 2026 announcement will come by May 27.

**Why YES is likely (~82%):**
- The dominant pattern is announcement on the **4th Tuesday of May**, which in 2026 falls on **May 26** — clearly within the deadline
- In 3 of 4 recent years (2022, 2024, 2025), announcements landed right on the 4th Tuesday
- Even a 1-day slip to May 27 still counts (deadline is 23:59 UTC May 27)
- The Days of Play landing page already has a "2026" page title, and industry outlets unanimously expect the event the week of May 26

**Why NO is possible (~18%):**
- In 2023, Sony delayed the entire event — announcing May 29 with the sale starting June 2. A similar delay in 2026 would push the announcement past the deadline
- 4 data points is a small sample; the "4th Tuesday" pattern could break for business reasons we can't observe
- The raw historical miss rate (2/4 years after May 27) would suggest higher uncertainty, but this doesn't account for the favorable calendar alignment in 2026
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will Sony officially announce the start date of PlayStation Days of Play 2026 on the PlayStation Blog by May 27, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43534)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if, between May 1, 2026 and 23:59 UTC on May 27, 2026, Sony publishes a post on the official [PlayStation Blog](https://blog.playstation.com/) that explicitly announces "Days of Play 2026" along with at least one specific calendar start date for the event. The post must come from the official PlayStation Blog domain (blog.playstation.com). Otherwise, the question resolves NO. If the PlayStation Blog is unavailable for verification, [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) reporting on such a PlayStation Blog announcement may be used as a fallback.
fine_print: Updates to the playstation.com Days of Play landing page alone do not count — the trigger is a PlayStation Blog post. Announcements made solely via PlayStation's social media accounts (X/Twitter, YouTube, Instagram) without a corresponding PlayStation Blog post do not count. The blog post must reference the 2026 edition specifically and provide a start date (an end date is not required).

factors.description: Days of Play has been an annual event every year since ~2017, always announced on PlayStation Blog. 100% historical occurrence rate. Landing page already updated for 2026.
Calendar alignment: The 4th Tuesday of May 2026 is May 26, perfectly within the May 27 deadline. In 3/4 recent years (2022, 2024, 2025), announcements fell on the 4th Tuesday of May.
Industry outlets unanimously expect event week of May 26, based on 'last Wednesday of May' pattern. Multiple credible sources (PlayStation LifeStyle, Startselect, G3Geek) all pointing to same window.

2023 outlier risk: In 2023, announcement was May 29 (after May 27 deadline equivalent) with sale starting June 2. This happened once in 4 years, giving ~20-25% base rate for a delay past the deadline.
Even a 1-day slip from May 26 to May 27 still resolves YES. The deadline is end of May 27 UTC, and blog posts typically publish at 5 AM PDT (12 PM UTC). Extra buffer.

input.anchor: Historical base rate: In 4 years of data (2022-2025), Days of Play blog announcement fell on or before May 27 in 2 of 4 years (50%). However, this is misleading because the calendar differs each year — the announcement is tied to the 4th Tuesday of May, which falls on different dates. In 2026, the 4th Tuesday is May 26, making it structurally more likely to fall within the deadline.
input.assessment:
The question asks whether Sony will publish a Days of Play 2026 announcement on the PlayStation Blog by May 27, 2026.

STRONG YES signals:
- Days of Play is an established annual tradition (~9 years running), always announced on PlayStation Blog
- The Days of Play landing page has already been updated with "2026" in the title
- The 4th Tuesday of May 2026 is May 26, fitting the dominant pattern perfectly
- Even if announcement slips to May 27, it still counts (deadline is end of May 27 UTC)
- Industry consensus points to event starting week of May 26

NO scenario:
- A 2023-style delay where the sale starts in early June instead of late May, pushing the announcement to May 28+
- 2023 was the only year in recent history where this happened (1/4 = 25%)
- But in 2023, the 4th Tuesday was May 23, making a May 29 announcement a full 6-day offset
- In 2026, even a similar 6-day offset from the 4th Tuesday (May 26) would land on June 1 — that's the NO scenario

The calendar alignment strongly favors YES in 2026. The 4th Tuesday (May 26) + 1 day buffer (May 27) = 2 days within deadline. For the announcement to miss, Sony would need to delay beyond their typical pattern by more than they have in any recent year except 2023.
input.calibration_notes: Raw historical base rate (2/4 years ≤ May 27) = 50%, but this ignores the calendar structure. When adjusted for the 4th-Tuesday pattern, the probability is significantly higher in 2026 because the 4th Tuesday falls on May 26. The 82% estimate reflects: high confidence in the event happening (~97%), very high confidence in blog announcement (~98%), and ~85% confidence in timing within deadline given the favorable calendar.
input.key_uncertainties: Main uncertainty is whether 2026 follows the dominant pattern (4th Tuesday announcement) or has a 2023-like delay. Secondary uncertainty: could Sony change their announcement strategy (e.g., skip blog post, announce only via social media)? This seems very unlikely given consistent practice.
input.tool_audit: Research tool: Very helpful, provided comprehensive historical data with exact dates and URLs. Web search: Confirmed no announcement yet, found corroborating industry speculation. Metaculus question fetch: Confirmed question details. All tools performed well with no failures.
input.update_triggers: 1. A PlayStation Blog post appearing before May 27 → YES confirmed. 2. Sony announcing Days of Play via social media only with no blog post → would need to assess if blog post follows. 3. No announcement by May 25 → would slightly lower probability but still expect May 26 announcement. 4. An announcement on May 28+ → NO confirmed.
input.process_reflection: The research sub-agent was excellent here — it went directly to the PlayStation Blog RSS feed and tag pages to get authoritative data on historical announcement dates, which is exactly what was needed. The web search corroborated that no announcement has been made yet. The code sandbox was useful for calendar calculations to understand the day-of-week pattern. No significant friction in the process. The decomposition into calendar-based analysis (4th Tuesday pattern) was the key insight that moved the estimate from a naive 50% base rate to a more informed ~82%.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
## Checks Performed

### Resolution Criteria
The agent fetched the Metaculus question page and engaged with the specific resolution criteria: PlayStation Blog post (not landing page), must reference "Days of Play 2026" with a specific start date, by 23:59 UTC May 27. The factors and assessment correctly distinguish between blog posts, landing page updates, and social media announcements. ✓

### Hallucinated Evidence
All key factual claims are grounded in the research trace:
- **Historical dates** (Factor 1, 2, 4): The research tool returned exact blog post URLs with dates — May 24 (2022), May 29 (2023), May 28 (2024), May 27 (2025). These match the factors. ✓
- **4th Tuesday pattern** (Factor 2): The agent's code execution computed 4th Tuesdays for each year and verified the pattern holds in 3/4 years. May 26, 2026 as the 4th Tuesday is arithmetically correct. ✓
- **Landing page updated** (Factor 1): Confirmed by Metaculus background info in the trace. ✓
- **Industry outlets** (Factor 3): Web search results from PlayStation LifeStyle, Startselect, G3Geek all confirm speculation about week of May 26. ✓
- **No announcement yet** confirmed via PlayStation Blog RSS feed check. ✓

### Factor 4 Wording
Factor 4 says "2023 outlier" happened "once in 4 years" giving "~20-25% base rate for a delay past the deadline." The agent's own code shows 2/4 years had announcements after May 27 (2023: May 29, 2024: May 28). However, the factor specifically refers to a *pattern-breaking* delay (2023-style, where the announcement was 6 days after the 4th Tuesday), not merely being after May 27. In 2024, the announcement was ON the 4th Tuesday (May 28) — the pattern held, it just happened to fall after the deadline due to calendar. This distinction is valid and the agent's overall analysis (anchor, counterargument) correctly notes the 2/4 raw miss rate. Minor wording ambiguity but not misleading in context.

### Double-Counting
Factor 3 (industry outlets expect week of May 26) partially overlaps with Factor 2 (calendar alignment) — industry speculation extrapolates from the same historical pattern. The agent's counterargument explicitly acknowledges this: "Industry speculation is just extrapolating historical patterns, adding no independent information." Factor 3's logit is only +0.5, so the impact is minimal (~1-2pp on final probability). Not material enough to flag.

### Wrong-Direction Factors
All factor signs are correct: positive for YES-supporting evidence, negative for the 2023 outlier risk. ✓

### Contradictory Assessment
The assessment narrative aligns with the factors. The agent correctly identifies that the 4th Tuesday pattern strongly favors YES in 2026, while acknowledging the 2023 delay risk as the main NO scenario. ✓

### Counterargument Quality
Strong. The agent constructs a genuine alternative story: 50% raw miss rate, pattern-matching on too few data points, business factors unknown, landing page signal is weak, industry speculation adds no independent information. This is a real argument a smart disagreer would make. ✓

### Confidence Assessment
0.50 confidence is appropriately calibrated for a forecast built on 4 data points with a clear outlier. ✓

### Probability Assessment
My independent estimate: ~78-85%. The 4th Tuesday pattern (3/4 adherence) maps to May 26 in 2026, within the deadline. Even a May 27 announcement counts. The main risk is a 2023-style full-week delay (~15-20%). The agent's 82% sits squarely in my range. No meaningful disagreement.

### Anchor Divergence
The raw base rate anchor is 50% (2/4 years on or before May 27). The final probability is 82%, a 32pp departure. However, this is well-justified by the strongest factor: in 2026, the 4th Tuesday falls on May 26, structurally inside the deadline. The 2/4 miss rate includes 2024 (May 28) where the 4th Tuesday itself fell after the deadline — a calendar artifact, not a pattern deviation. The departure from the raw anchor is driven by legitimate structural analysis, not narrative accumulation. ✓

### Tool Audit
Agent claims all tools performed well with no failures. Ground truth: 9 calls, 0 errors. Consistent. ✓

### Worldview Consistency
No worldview forecasts or research entries contradict the agent's claims. The two worldview research entries are the same ones used in the trace. ✓

No errors found warranting a warn or fail.
